data.title: ECB warns against quick rate cuts as tariff impact remains uncertain
data.description:
ECB says it needs more time to understand how U. S. tariffs will affect inflation.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ecb-warns-against-quick-rate-cuts/

data.title: Fed's Kashkari: Economic slowdown may be worse than reality
data.description: Fed Chair Neel Kashkari said the risk of an unexpected downturn in the labor market outweighs the risk of a slight pickup in inflation. We may perceive the economic slowdown as more severe than it actually is. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bank of New York Mellon: Focusing on stablecoin infrastructure, not considering issuing its own tokens for now
data.description: Bank of New York Mellon (BNY Mellon) executives said during a recent earnings call on Thursday that the bank has brought forward several blockchain-related investments to 2025, including those supporting real-world asset settlement and tokenized payments. Executives attributed the accelerated pace to a more "positive" regulatory environment and improved market conditions. When asked whether BNY Mellon plans to issue its own stablecoin, CEO Robin Vince did not provide a definitive answer, but stated that the bank's strategy focuses on supporting the broader ecosystem rather than launching its own branded token. A BNY Mellon-branded stablecoin could serve institutional use cases, such as settling tokenized assets or facilitating intraday liquidity. However, for now, the bank appears more focused on building flexible infrastructure than launching products. "We're in the business of building infrastructure and enabling capital markets, and working with stablecoins and supporting other stablecoins is core to our strategy," Vince said during the call. Furthermore, the company said it has reallocated approximately $500 million in cost savings this year to growth initiatives, including in digital assets and artificial intelligence. These efficiency gains are the result of internal process optimizations and have not significantly expanded the company's expense base. (CoinDesk)

data.title: 21Shares Seeks Approval for Leveraged HYPE ETF
data.description: According to PANews, 21Shares has filed an application for a 2x leveraged HYPE ETF, as revealed by Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas.

data.title: A whale opened a $100 million ETH short position with 25x leverage, and now has a floating profit of $638,000
data.description: According to on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, the address starting with 0x0b5 has opened a 25x short position in ETH worth $100 million in the past 10 hours, with a current floating profit of $638,000. In addition, he also opened a short position in SOL and a long position in ENA, with an overall floating profit of $804,000. The specific positions are as follows: - ETH 25x short position: $100 million (25,887.39 coins), opening price $3,926.95 - SOL 20x short position: $5.71 million (30,806.13 coins), opening price $186.256 - ENA 10x long position: $580,000 (1.44 million coins), opening price $0.4

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Recent market pullback may be driven by native cryptocurrency investors
data.description: JPMorgan analysts suggest that last week's sharp crypto market correction, accompanied by large-scale liquidations, may have been driven by native crypto investors, rather than institutional or retail ETF holders. The report shows that spot Bitcoin ETFs, popular with traditional retail investors, showed "almost no signs of large-scale liquidations." From October 10th to 14th, Bitcoin ETFs experienced net outflows of $220 million, representing only 0.14% of total assets under management; Ethereum ETFs saw net outflows of $370 million, representing 1.23%. Meanwhile, CME Bitcoin futures saw almost no liquidation, while CME Ethereum futures saw significant deleveraging, possibly reflecting momentum traders "substantially reducing their risk exposure." In contrast, perpetual futures, favored by native crypto traders, saw significant deleveraging, with open interest in Bitcoin and Ethereum perpetual contracts falling by approximately 40%, a drop that outpaced the price declines of both assets. Analysts believe that native crypto investors were the primary driver of last week's market correction, while non-native crypto investors largely remained on the sidelines. (The Block)

data.title: Tether faces a lawsuit over the freezing of $44.7 million in stablecoins, with the plaintiff claiming the illegal freezing of its tokens resulted in a significant loss of investment opportunities.
data.description: In April of this year, stablecoin issuer Tether froze $44.7 million in USDT at the request of Bulgarian police. Now, Texas-based Riverstone Consulting has filed a lawsuit, alleging that Tether illegally froze its tokens and caused it to miss out on significant investment opportunities. According to the complaint filed in the Southern District Court of New York, Tether froze assets in eight offline wallets controlled by Riverstone on April 4th. The company claims that Tether's freezing of assets solely based on a request from the Bulgarian local police was "procedurally flawed" and failed to follow the formal procedures required by Bulgaria's International Mutual Legal Assistance Treaty, which requires the exchange of filings through central authorities and foreign affairs liaison channels. The complaint alleges that when Riverstone contacted Tether, they were told to communicate directly with the Bulgarian police, but the police have not responded. Tether's stablecoin currently has over $180 billion in circulation, and as of September 15th, Riverstone had cooperated with global law enforcement agencies to freeze $3.2 billion in USDT. (dlnews)

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks fell across the board at the close of US stocks, with TRON falling 11.65%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of US trading, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.17%, the S&P 500 fell 0.25%, and the Nasdaq fell 0.23%. The crypto sector fell across the board, with TRON down 11.65%, BTCM down 4.72%, and CRCL down 3.41%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, including US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Charles Schwab Plans to Launch Bitcoin and Cryptocurrency Trading Services in 2026
data.description: According to Foresight News, Charles Schwab is set to introduce Bitcoin spot and cryptocurrency trading services in 2026. This move marks a significant step for the financial services company as it expands its offerings in the digital asset space. The decision aligns with the growing interest and demand for cryptocurrency investments among retail and institutional investors. Charles Schwab's entry into the crypto market is expected to enhance accessibility and provide more options for clients seeking exposure to digital currencies. The company aims to leverage its established reputation and resources to offer secure and efficient trading services. As the cryptocurrency industry continues to evolve, Charles Schwab's initiative reflects the broader trend of traditional financial institutions embracing digital assets.

data.title: Bitcoin Integration Campaign Gains Momentum Amid Privacy Concerns
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a campaign advocating for the integration of Bitcoin into the privacy-focused messaging app Signal is gaining traction. Spearheaded by several Bitcoin enthusiasts, including Jack Dorsey, the initiative aims to incorporate Bitcoin payments within Signal using the Cashu protocol. Dorsey, a prominent figure in the tech industry, expressed his support by retweeting a post from pseudonymous Bitcoin developer Cashu, who is leading the campaign. The proposed integration would utilize Cashu’s Chaumian Ecash solution, which promises to enable private Bitcoin transactions within Signal, a widely trusted encryption messenger.

The campaign has garnered endorsements from notable figures in the Bitcoin community, including developer Peter Todd. Todd criticized Signal’s existing crypto payment solution, MobileCoin, for its centralized nature and limited accessibility, advocating for Bitcoin as a more viable alternative. Signal introduced MobileCoin in April 2021, but it has faced scrutiny for its small validator set. Other supporters of the Bitcoin for Signal campaign include pseudonymous Bitcoin developer Calle and Satoshi Labs co-founder Pavol Rusnak. With Signal boasting approximately 70 million monthly active users, successful Bitcoin integration could transform the app into a significant platform for peer-to-peer transactions.

Despite the enthusiasm surrounding the campaign, critics have raised concerns about Bitcoin’s suitability for a privacy-focused app like Signal. Some argue that Bitcoin’s public blockchain may compromise user privacy, suggesting alternatives like Monero and Zcash, which offer enhanced privacy features. José Pedro Sousa, an engineer at Aztec Network, questioned the use of a public blockchain for a privacy-centric chat application. Techlore, a digital rights advocacy group, also warned that Bitcoin integration could jeopardize Signal users' privacy. While Cashu offers a privacy-preserving solution for Bitcoin, similar applications have struggled to achieve widespread success.

The push for Bitcoin-backed private messaging apps coincides with the European Union’s consideration of the controversial Chat Control law. This proposed legislation would require messaging apps, including encrypted ones like Signal and WhatsApp, to provide authorities access to private messages to detect child abuse material, potentially undermining end-to-end encryption. Germany has opposed the proposal, citing constitutional concerns over mass scanning of private messages. The vote on the law has been postponed, with another vote scheduled for early December, indicating that the debate over privacy and encryption is far from over.

data.title: US real estate investment firm Cardone Capital disclosed an increase of 200 BTC
data.description: Grant Cardone, CEO of US real estate investment company Cardone Capital, posted on the X platform this morning that Cardone Capital purchased another 200 BTC, following its purchase of 300 BTC last week.

data.title: A certain whale has completely given back its previous $140 million short position profit, and its current position has a floating loss of $4.69 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, a whale account previously profited $2.683 million from a $140 million short position, but has since relinquished all of that profit. Last night, the whale significantly increased its long position, bringing its total holdings to $162 million, including approximately $84.67 million in BTC and $77.43 million in ETH. Currently, the account's overall floating loss is approximately $4.69 million.

data.title: CME Group Plans to Launch Sports and Economic Event-Linked Financial Contracts
data.description: According to Foresight News, Bloomberg reports that CME Group Inc. is planning to introduce financial contracts linked to sports events and economic indicators by the end of the year. The Chicago-based exchange intends to offer these new contracts to the public through futures commission merchants, including a joint venture with FanDuel, a subsidiary of Flutter Entertainment. Additionally, CME Group may make these contracts available on other similar platforms, thereby opening them to traditional retail investment brokerage firms. A representative from CME Group declined to comment on the plans, noting that they are still subject to change.

data.title: OpenSea: Wave 1 rewards have been distributed
data.description: OpenSea announced the distribution of Wave 1 on the X platform, with users now able to unlock their rewards in the official treasure chest. Previously, Wave 1, the final event before the OpenSea TGE, had a total reward value of $12.2 million. Wave 2 launched on October 15th.

data.title: Newsmax, a US-listed company, plans to invest $5 million to create a BTC and TRUMP reserve
data.description: Newsmax, a publicly traded US media company, announced it will establish a digital asset reserve centered around Bitcoin and the Trump meme token (TRUMP). The company's board of directors has approved the plan, which will allocate up to $5 million cumulatively to these two assets over the next year.

data.title: Florida Moves to Legalize Bitcoin Investments in State Funds
data.description:
Florida lawmakers have launched the 2026 legislative session with a proposal to integrate Bitcoin into the state’s official investment strategy. The measure, filed on October 15, could make Florida one of the first US states to manage digital assets as part of its public reserves. Lawmakers Push for Crypto Reserve Strategy The initiative, filed as House Bill 183 by Representative Webster Barnaby, authorizes the state’s Chief Financial Officer to allocate up to 10% of specific funds—including the General Revenue Fund and the Budget Stabilization Fund—into Bitcoin and other digital-asset products. The bill defines digital assets broadly, encompassing Bitcoin, tokenized securities, and NFTs
source: https://beincrypto.com/florida-moves-to-legalize-bitcoin-investments-in-state-funds/

data.title: Tether Nominates Executives to Juventus Board Amid Governance Changes
data.description: According to PANews, Tether has announced the nomination of its Deputy Chief Investment Officer, Zachary Lyons, and orthodontist Francesco Garino, a lifelong Juventus supporter, to the board of the football club. Tether stated that since its investment in the club, it has actively engaged with fans from Italy and around the world, receiving valuable feedback and calls for deeper collaboration. The company has proposed amendments to the club's charter, which will be voted on at the shareholders' meeting, aiming to adopt top-tier corporate governance and minority representation mechanisms. Following investments made in February and April, Tether holds a 10.7% stake in Juventus.

data.title: Oracle’s stock surges by 3% after Meta cloud deal confirmation and bullish AI revenue forecast
data.description:
Oracle stock surged 3% on Thursday, after the company confirmed a major cloud-computing partnership with Meta and issued a powerful new forecast for its artificial intelligence (AI) revenue growth. The announcement came during Oracle’s AI World conference in Las Vegas, where the company outlined how its AI-driven database business will expand sharply in the coming […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/oracles-stock-surges-by-3/

data.title: Fed's Kashkari Highlights Labor Market Risks Over Inflation Concerns
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve official Neel Kashkari has expressed concerns that the risk of an unexpected downturn in the labor market outweighs the risk of a slight increase in inflation. He suggested that the perceived severity of the economic slowdown might be greater than the actual situation.
